_enforcement_modes: block = deny non-compliant requests | warn = allow but flag | audit = allow + log only

restricted_software.name: telnet
restricted_software.reason: Telnet transmits credentials and session data in cleartext, violating the data-in-transit encryption standard.
restricted_software.alternative_note: Use SSH for encrypted remote shell access. It is on the approved baseline.

restricted_software.name: ftp
restricted_software.reason: Plain FTP sends credentials and files unencrypted and is disallowed for any data transfer.
restricted_software.alternative_note: SFTP over SSH, or FTPS with TLS, satisfies the encryption-in-transit control.

restricted_software.name: rsh
restricted_software.reason: Legacy r-services perform no encryption and rely on trust-host authentication, which is prohibited.
restricted_software.alternative_note: SSH replaces all r-command functionality securely.

restricted_software.name: teamviewer
restricted_software.reason: Third-party remote-access tools bypass the corporate identity perimeter and are not permitted on provisioned instances.
restricted_software.alternative_note: Identity-Aware Proxy provides audited, SSO-backed remote access without exposing a port.

restricted_software.name: ngrok
restricted_software.reason: Reverse tunnels expose internal services to the public internet outside of network governance.
restricted_software.alternative_note: Use a managed load balancer with IAP for controlled external exposure.

restricted_software.name: tor
restricted_software.reason: Anonymizing networks defeat egress monitoring and are prohibited on corporate infrastructure.
restricted_software.alternative_note: No compliant alternative. If anonymized testing is required, request an isolated research enclave via InfoSec.

restricted_software.name: php5
restricted_software.reason: PHP 5.x is end-of-life and receives no security patches, violating the supported-software standard.
restricted_software.alternative_note: Use a currently supported PHP 8.x release.

restricted_software.name: python2
restricted_software.reason: Python 2 reached end-of-life in 2020 and is no longer patched.
restricted_software.alternative_note: python3 is on the approved baseline.

restricted_software.name: redis-unauthenticated
restricted_software.reason: Redis exposed without a password and bound to a public interface is a frequent breach vector.
restricted_software.suggested_alternative: redis (with requirepass + private IP) or Cloud Memorystore
restricted_software.alternative_note: Redis is approved only when authenticated and bound to a private network.

restricted_software.name: nmap
restricted_software.reason: Network scanners are restricted to approved security testing and require explicit authorization.
restricted_software.alternative_note: Permitted on approval for designated pentest engagements only.

prohibited_categories.reason: Cryptocurrency mining is an unauthorized use of compute resources and is hard-blocked.
Peer-to-peer file sharing carries legal and malware risk and is not permitted.
Offensive security frameworks are restricted to the sanctioned red-team environment.
